title: Emma's Big Feelings
synopsis: Emma experiences different big feelings throughout her day and learns that all feelings are okay with help from her loving family.

pages.text: Emma wakes up grumpy. Her socks feel funny. Her cereal is too cold. Everything makes her MAD!

illustration.description: Emma with straight blonde hair in a ponytail, fair skin, bright blue eyes, rosy cheeks sits at breakfast table with angry red swirls surrounding her, frowning face, crossed arms, abstract angry emotion background with jagged red shapes
illustration.alt: Emma looking angry at breakfast with red swirling emotions around her

pages.text: Mama gives Emma a big hug. 'It's okay to feel mad sometimes,' she says softly. The red angry feeling starts to fade away.

illustration.description: Emma with straight blonde hair in a ponytail, fair skin, bright blue eyes, rosy cheeks being hugged by her mother, red swirls slowly dissolving into softer pink clouds, warm embrace, calming emotion visualization background
illustration.alt: Emma being comforted by her mother as angry feelings fade

pages.text: At the park, Emma's favorite toy breaks. She feels very SAD. Blue tears roll down her cheeks like little raindrops.

illustration.description: Emma with straight blonde hair in a ponytail, fair skin, bright blue eyes, rosy cheeks sitting on park bench crying, sad blue rain falling around her, broken toy in lap, abstract blue teardrop shapes in background, Inside Out inspired sadness visualization
illustration.alt: Emma crying at the park with blue rain representing her sadness

pages.text: Daddy sits beside Emma. 'Crying helps wash sad feelings away,' he explains. He holds her hand until she feels better.

illustration.description: Emma with straight blonde hair in a ponytail, fair skin, bright blue eyes, rosy cheeks holding hands with her father on park bench, blue rain gradually clearing to lighter blue mist, comforting presence, gentle emotion background transitioning from dark to light blue
illustration.alt: Emma's father comforting her as the blue sadness begins to clear

pages.text: At dinner, Grandma visits! Emma feels HAPPY. Golden sunshine fills her heart and makes her giggle with joy.

illustration.description: Emma with straight blonde hair in a ponytail, fair skin, bright blue eyes, rosy cheeks jumping up excitedly at dinner table, happy yellow sunshine radiating around her, big smile, golden light rays and cheerful star shapes in background, Inside Out inspired joy visualization
illustration.alt: Emma radiating happiness with yellow sunshine emotions around her

pages.text: Emma hugs her family tight. She learned that all feelings are okay - mad, sad, and happy. Love makes everything better.

illustration.description: Emma with straight blonde hair in a ponytail, fair skin, bright blue eyes, rosy cheeks in group hug with family, rainbow of gentle emotion colors swirling peacefully around them - soft reds, blues, and yellows blending harmoniously, warm loving background with heart shapes
illustration.alt: Emma hugging her family surrounded by a rainbow of peaceful emotions

metadata.illustrationStyle: emotion mapped colors, angry red swirls, sad blue rain, happy yellow sunshine, abstract emotion backgrounds, Inside Out inspired, feeling visualization, color psychology

questions.question: What color shows Emma's mad feelings?
questions.explanation: Red swirls showed Emma's angry mad feelings in the morning.

questions.question: Who hugged Emma when she felt mad?
questions.explanation: Mama gave Emma a big hug and told her it's okay to feel mad sometimes.

questions.question: What made Emma feel sad at the park?
questions.explanation: Emma's favorite toy broke at the park, which made her feel very sad.

questions.question: What did Emma learn about feelings?
questions.explanation: Emma learned that all feelings - mad, sad, and happy - are okay to have.
